rules_evaluated.evidence: No hits on banned terms: honest, delve, landscape-as-verb, importantly, crucial, substrate, leverage-as-verb, synergize, paradigm shift

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
rules_evaluated.evidence: Practice (noun) appears at line 106 in maintainer block, exempt. Body prose uses 'exercise' (lines 5, 8, 13, 94, 96) which is approved. No violation of practice-noun ban.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No stale markers, no fixed retrospectives
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body clean. Maintainer block citations to session date and prior edits are permitted under source-date and frontmatter exceptions.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Register match — Bootstrap voice trio (Godin × Sutherland × Siilasmaa)
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 5–14: peer-warm direct voice ('what you do / what happens / the point'). Line 13–25: Sutherland reframe embedded in Subagent 3 design ('Counterintuitive reframer'). Lines 82–90: optimistic action framing ('Hold the doubt. Name it to yourself' + forward bridge 'Module 5 builds the tools'). Tone is conversational, direct, warm. No L&D-coach or consultancy-deck register drift.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training creator's name banned + Author-'we' ban
rules_evaluated.evidence: No mention of Antti, 'the creator', 'the author' (person-noun), 'course creator' in body (lines 1–91). No author-we (first-person-plural to training org). Uses 'you' (student) and impersonal training-subject ('The previous exercise', 'Module 5'). Maintainer exempt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always 'you', never 'the student'; teacher framing out of body
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body uses 'you' for student action throughout (lines 5, 18, 25, 29, 82–86). No teacher-facing mechanics in body. No 'the student' above line 92.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body
rules_evaluated.evidence: Subagent: defined operationally at line 23 in context ('Claude Code calls them **subagents**'). Stance: concrete word, not jargon. Synthesis: operational. Rumelt/Martin/Sutherland: named as credited frameworks, operationally in use. Strategy kernel: appears in prompt and maintainer credit (line 99). No maintainer-exclusive jargon leaking to body prose.

Line 71: 'Heads-up: the answer often comes back with a longer issue list' is honest framing of expected output shape, not reassurance. Line 86: 'That feeling is correct' affirms the student's valid doubt. No reassurance-shaped hedge (no 'it's OK if', 'no penalty', 'not a downgrade').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing NOT in student-facing body
rules_evaluated.evidence: No positioning-defense language ('not aspirational', 'not vendor', 'unlike consultancy', 'real practitioners', 'grounded in actual'). Body stays pedagogical — three stances, framework application, doubt-holding — without importing curriculum's anti-vendor-story positioning.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Watch out for planting jargon too early
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module 3 placement: 'subagent' introduced with operational definition at line 23 (not pre-planted). 'Stance' is concrete, earned in use. 'Rumelt's strategy kernel' is a named framework credited in prompt and maintainer, operationally deployed. No arc-ahead jargon injected.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use
rules_evaluated.evidence: Subagent: earned at line 23 in operational context. Memory: disambiguated at line 21 ('the layer the previous exercise integrated'). No unearned technical terms in body. Module references spelled out (Module 5, Module 3) per rule.

No embedding, inference, prompt engineering, orchestration, schema, statistical default, retrieval, fine-tuning. Body uses engineer-accessible language (three stances, framework, diagnosis, guiding policy, coherent actions are operational concepts, not jargon).

Agent used for autonomous work: line 23 'each runs with its own context window, works in parallel'; line 25 'main session reads them'. Claude used for invocation: lines 29, 50, 75 'Ask Claude to...'. No conflation; vocabulary clean and precise.
Fences at lines 33–45, 54–66, 77–79: no [BRACKETS] or [path] placeholders. Prompts reference 'my curated memory/', 'module-3/stances/', './crux.md' — all pasteable without student substitution.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 31: 'Ask Claude to spawn three subagents with different stances, then apply Rumelt's kernel to synthesize their notes back into `./crux.md`.' Lead-in present, semantic. Line 50: semantic lead-in present. Line 75: lead-in present.
Shape: lead-in, blank, **Prompt** label, blank, fenced block. Both main prompts follow canonical shape. Optional prompt follows same shape.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Runtime-fork prompt blocks: paired divs, shared chip
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 27–47: `<div class="rt-code">` wrapping Code prompt (lines 33–45). Lines 48–68: `<div class="rt-cowork">` wrapping Cowork prompt (lines 54–66). Both share `**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*` chip. Mechanical content differs (subagent ↔ agent); pedagogical content identical. Correct fork pattern.

Module 3 closer (maintainer line 96). Lines 82–90 name the mood: 'That feeling is correct. Hold the doubt. Name it to yourself. Don't fix it here.' Mood is held-doubt / unsettled-competence, not resolved. Line 90 bridges to Module 5: 'Module 5 builds the tools to name what's off systematically.' Mood contract honored.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode
rules_evaluated.evidence: Title: 'Three minds, one synthesis' (discipline: multi-perspective synthesis via framework). Big Idea at line 9 focuses on discipline (kernel-as-spine). No failure-mode framing. Positive throughout.

summary: Writing class PASS. All compendium rules clear. Body prose clean, peer-warm, direct. Prompts well-formed, fully pasteable. Fenced blocks follow candidate shape. Runtime forks (Code/Cowork) correct. No violations. Ready to ship.
